other_observers: At this time at least 150 birders from 4 states
duration: At least once a day for the last two weeks (ten hours)
description: Brenda and I found the bird in the AM of 18 January. We have had a lot of experience with this species or we could has just passed it off as another American Robin. We checked ebird records and the three weeks prior to finding the bird, 11 parties birded that area so one can only guess how long the Rufous-backed Robin may have been at Denny Lake. Rufous back and wings set this species off from American Robins. It is smaller, has a different flight pattern (quick and direct), yellowish bill and orange eyering and lacks the eye arcs of the American Robin. Head has a gray tone.
photographer: Photographer: Brenda Wright, Brenda Wright, Brenda Wright
